  "findings": [
    "Kinked and flattened EMT bend reduces internal cross-sectional area",
    "Damaged conduit wall compromises structural integrity and wire-pulling safety"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut out and remove the kinked section of EMT",
    "Bend a new piece of EMT using the correct bender size and technique to avoid flattening",
    "Reinstall the conduit run using approved couplings"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC Article 358.24 requires that bends in EMT be made so that the conduit is not damaged and its internal diameter is not effectively reduced. A kinked or flattened bend violates this requirement, restricts wire fill capacity, and can damage conductor insulation during wire pulling.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Article 358.24",
    "NEC Chapter 9"
  ]
}
